name: CAPRICOR THERAPEUTICS, INC.
summary: Capricor Therapeutics, Inc. is a Delaware-based biotechnology company focused on developing cell and exosome-based therapeutics for Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD) and other diseases with significant unmet medical need. Its lead product candidate, Deramiocel, is a cardiosphere-derived cell (CDC) therapy with a BLA under FDA review (PDUFA date: August 22, 2026). The company also advances its exosome platform (StealthX) for infectious diseases, monogenic diseases, and other indications through collaborations with NIH, DoD, Johns Hopkins, and others.

products.name: Deramiocel
products.description: Cardiosphere-derived cell (CDC) therapy for Duchenne muscular dystrophy targeting cardiac and skeletal muscle complications via immunomodulatory, anti-inflammatory, pro-angiogenic, and anti-fibrotic mechanisms.

products.name: StealthX
products.description: Exosome-based therapeutic and vaccine platform leveraging RNA biology, protein engineering, and targeted delivery for infectious diseases, monogenic diseases, and other indications.

partnerships.description: Exclusive distributor for Deramiocel in DMD in the United States and Japan.
Collaboration supporting exosome platform research and development.

partnerships.partner_name: National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID)
partnerships.description: Collaboration supporting exosome-based research, including infectious disease applications.
External collaboration supporting exosome platform research.

partnerships.partner_name: U.S. Army Institute of Surgical Research (USAISR)

partnerships.description: External collaboration supporting exosome platform research.
